full_content:
Cross-cutting requirements registry schema, location, and update procedure

The cross-cutting requirements registry MUST conform to the following machine-enforceable contract:

LOCATION (binding):
- Canonical registry file: `.claude/rules/cross-cutting-requirements.toml`
- Loaded at session start by the harness rule loader.
- Tracked in git (visible to both Prime Builder and Codex Loyal Opposition).

SCHEMA (per registry entry; TOML table form):
Each entry under [requirements.<id>] MUST carry:

  id                 (string, required) — Stable identifier matching the governing rule's ID prefix convention. Examples: "CCR-PROJECT-ROOT-BOUNDARY-001", "CCR-CANONICAL-TERMINOLOGY-001", "CCR-SPEC-LINKAGE-GATE-001".
  title              (string, required) — Short human-readable title.
  rule_text          (string, required) — Verbatim quote of the rule from the source rule file. MUST be byte-exact (whitespace and punctuation preserved) so doctor-time drift can be detected.
  source             (table, required) — Provenance:
                       file        (string, required) — relative path to rule source file (e.g., ".claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md").
                       section     (string, optional) — heading or anchor.
                       delib_id    (string, required) — owner-decision DELIB that authorizes this rule.
                       gov_id      (string, optional) — governance spec ID.
                       adr_id      (string, optional) — architecture decision record ID.
                       dcl_id      (string, optional) — design constraint ID.
  file_scope         (array of glob strings, required) — File-path globs this rule applies to. Empty array means rule applies universally to any file/operation.
  fast_check         (table, required) — Write-time check definition:
                       kind        (enum: "regex" | "glob_absent" | "glob_present" | "ast_query" | "script", required)
                       pattern     (string, required for regex/glob/ast_query)
                       script      (string, required for kind=script — relative path to executable check script)
                       message     (string, required) — Block message shown when violation detected.
  deep_check         (table, required) — Codex review-time check:
                       prompt      (string, required) — Review prompt for Codex to evaluate against the proposal.
                       expected    (enum: "pass" | "no_violation_found" | "explicit_compliance_evidence", required) — What Codex must conclude for the requirement to pass.
                       evidence_required (boolean, required) — If true, the proposal MUST cite explicit compliance evidence (paths, line numbers, or test references); silent compliance does not pass.
  exceptions         (array of tables, optional) — Per-proposal exceptions:
                       delib_id    (string, required) — Authorizing waiver DELIB.
                       scope       (string, required) — Exact bridge file pattern, work item ID, or release version the exception covers.
                       expiry      (string, required) — When the exception lapses (release version OR work item ID OR ISO date). Never "indefinite".
                       residual_risk (string, required) — Plain-language risk if the exception is exploited.

UPDATE PROCEDURE (binding):
- Adding a new requirement: requires owner-approved DELIB cited in the entry's source.delib_id, plus a bridge proposal for the registry update (subject to formal-artifact-approval gate). The DELIB's content must be the verbatim owner statement that motivated the requirement.
- Removing a requirement: requires owner-approved DELIB explicitly stating the supersession or retirement, and a bridge proposal documenting why the requirement no longer applies.
- Editing rule_text: MUST trigger drift-detection — if the source rule file changes, the registry's rule_text MUST be updated within the same bridge cycle (or the rule file change is itself out-of-spec).
- Adding/modifying fast_check or deep_check: requires the change to be paired with regression test coverage proving the check correctly identifies a known violation.

DOCTOR INVARIANTS (testable):
- `gt project doctor` MUST verify:
  (a) every entry's source.file exists and contains rule_text byte-exact (drift detection — promotes any drift to ERROR severity);
  (b) every entry's fast_check.kind is in the valid enum;
  (c) every entry's exceptions[].expiry is parseable + not expired (expired exceptions promote to ERROR);
  (d) registry file itself parses as valid TOML with no missing required fields per the schema above.
- Doctor MUST be runnable from CI (release-candidate gate) and from local developer machine (Windows + Linux).

Rationale:
This DCL specifies the machine-checkable contract that GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 depends on. Without a fixed schema and known location, the enforcement layers (PreToolUse hook, Codex review) cannot consistently consult the registry. Doctor-time drift detection ensures the registry stays in sync with the source rule files (preventing the failure mode where rules drift but the registry is stale).

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT. Source intent: Registry contract for the cross-cutting-requirements enforcement system; companion DCL to GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001.
